root_text.sn22.135:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.135:2.1: “Idhāvuso, assutavā puthujjano rūpaṁ nappajānāti, rūpasamudayaṁ nappajānāti, rūpanirodhaṁ nappajānāti, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.135:2.5: viññāṇaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇasamudayaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇanirodhaṁ nappajānāti, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.sn22.135:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ mahākoṭṭhikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.135:4.1: “Idhāvuso, sutavā ariyasāvako rūpaṁ pajānāti, rūpasamudayaṁ pajānāti, rūpanirodhaṁ pajānāti, rūpanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn22.135:4.5: viññāṇaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇasamudayaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇanirodhaṁ pajānāti, viññāṇanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ pajānāti.

translation_text.sn22.135:1.3: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘ignorance’.
translation_text.sn22.135:1.5: And how is an ignorant person defined?”
translation_text.sn22.135:2.1: “Reverend, it’s when an unlearned ordinary person doesn’t understand form, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.
translation_text.sn22.135:2.5: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.
translation_text.sn22.135:2.7: And this is how an ignorant person is defined.”
translation_text.sn22.135:3.1: When he said this, Venerable Sāriputta said to him:
translation_text.sn22.135:3.2: “Reverend Koṭṭhita, they speak of this thing called ‘knowledge’.
translation_text.sn22.135:3.4: And how is a knowledgeable person defined?”
translation_text.sn22.135:4.1: “Reverend, it’s when a learned noble disciple understands form, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.
translation_text.sn22.135:4.5: consciousness, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation.
translation_text.sn22.135:4.7: And this is how a knowledgeable person is defined.”
